sections.Abstract: Mila Kunis (born December 20, 1951) is an American biologist and the director of the Center for Biological Research and Development (CRIAR) at the University of California, Irvine. She is a recipient of the 2020 NASA Exceptional Space Medal.

Kunis is a member of the National Academy of Sciences and Arts and Sciences. She has served as a member of the National Academy of Sciences (20022). sections.Abstract: Titanic is a cruise ship built for the U.S. Navy by the American shipyard in the late 1940s. She was the first vessel to be powered by a diesel-electric turbine. She was named after the Greek goddess of thunderous winds.
sections.Construction:
Titanic was built in San Francisco, California, by the United States Navy under the yard number S.S. 531. In February 1943, she was transferred to the U.S. Navy and placed in service in March 1944 as USS William J. and SS William B. Watson, respectively. She was laid down as SS Great Lakes (Portsmouth) on 2 May 1944 at Newport News Shipyard, New York, United States.
Titanic was one of several ships built for the U.S. Navy in the early 1940s; however, she was never commissioned for service in World War II; her name was changed to "Titanic" after World War II, and she was renamed SS Great Lakes (now SS Great Lakes).
sections.Service history:
Titanic was launched on 9 August 1943, sponsored by Mrs. Henry M. "Bud" A. Stearns and registered in Washington, D.C., United States Navy (USN). She served on various roles with her sister ship, the USS Washington, which sank on 10 June 1943 when she collided with a nearby submarine-launched ballistic missile submarine USS Mount Vernon on 14 November 1943. She served as SS Great Lakes until 1950, when she was transferred to North Carolina for conversion to Maritime Commissioning (COD) 9 June 1960; and to Coast Guard Reserve Fleet (MARCOM) 15 December 1968 until her decommissioning in July 1982; she returned to service in 1981 but remained there until being retired from service on 1 January 1989.
Titanic was last seen off New Orleans on 29 July 1988, when she collided with a seaplane tender off New Orleans in Operation Overdrive during a collision course with a naval minefield near Port Canaveral River, Louisiana. Titanic received emergency medical attention after her recovery from an emergency medical facility in San Diego, Louisiana before being towed to another base at New Orleans on 19 January 1990 for scrapping repairs at Fort Lauderdale Island, Florida; and finally arrived back at Port Canaveral on 3 January 1991; then returned to San Diego where she re-entered her port of Stony Brook on 19 February 1994. She is currently undergoing repairs at Fort Lauderdale Island and Gulf of Mexico for conversion back into Port Canaveral for conversion into Port Canaveral before being scrapped in April 1993 when she was sold for conversion into Port Canaveral following repairs due to weather conditions that were not available at Port Canaveral after repairs which lasted until 2002, when she was returned to Port Canaveral as part of the Coast Guard Sealift Commandos Program (RTFOC) and transferred to the Coast Guard Sealift Commandos Program (MCCP), which operated out of Port Canaveral during 19901993.

sections.Abstract: Charles Manson (January 17, 1930  July 29, 2012) was an American physicist. He was awarded the John von Neumann Medal from the American Physical Society (APS) in 1975.
sections.Biography:

He was a professor of physics at the University of California, Berkeley and a researcher in the field of optical spectroscopy and spectroscopy.
He was elected to the National Academy of Engineering in 1976 for "contributions that include optical spectroscopic measurements that can be used to produce spectra with optical properties".

sections.Abstract: Ryan Reynolds (1 December 1857  30 May 1933) was an English-born American engineer, known for his research and teaching in the areas of engineering, mechanics, and mathematics. He was a professor of mechanical engineering at the University of Chicago from 1933 to 1938.
